Summary: The texts describe OLIG1/2, including OLIG2, as master regulators of oligodendrocyte development. Knocking out Olig2 in mice led to failure of oligodendroglial lineage generation. Specific deletion of Olig2 in OPCs caused hypomyelination by reducing oligodendrocyte differentiation. Ablation of Olig2 in immature oligodendrocytes induced oligodendrocyte maturation, myelination, and remyelination in mice. Overexpression of Olig2 in NSCs promoted generation of OPCs and mature oligodendrocytes in vitro and in vivo. In mouse NSCs, OPCs, and newly formed oligodendrocytes OLIG2 binds protein-coding and lncRNA genes in vivo. Motif analysis showed OLIG2 binding regions also contain motifs for other transcription factors. OLIG2 binds some bivalent (H3K27me3/H3K4me3) genes and AU-rich elements in the Olig2 mRNA 3′ UTR. In NSCs OLIG2 binds and represses lnc-OPC and likely maintains NSC self-renewal. In OPCs OLIG2 does not bind the lnc-OPC upstream region and lnc-OPC expression increases. Knockdown of lnc-OPC reduced OPC formation from NSCs. Across stages OLIG2 had peaks at 613 expressed lncRNAs and clustering suggested repression of 39 lncRNAs in NSCs and activation of nine in OPCs and NFOs, correlating with oligodendrocyte development, so OLIG2 potentially regulates differentiation by activating or repressing lncRNAs.
References: Wei, H., Dong, X., You, Y., Hai, B., Duran, R. C., Wu, X., Kharas, N., & Wu, J. Q. (2021). OLIG2 regulates lncRNAs and its own expression during oligodendrocyte lineage formation. BMC Biol, 19(1), 132. https://doi.org/10.1186/s12915-021-01057-6
